title: HELLP Syndrome
chief_complaint: I'm 32 weeks pregnant. I have severe pain in my right upper stomach, I feel nauseous, and I have a headache. I feel generally unwell, like I have the flu.

physical_exam.Abdomen: RUQ tenderness. Liver edge palpable (tender). No rebound. Fundus appropriate for dates.
physical_exam.Extremities: Mild edema. Hyperreflexia with clonus (preeclampsia spectrum).
physical_exam.Fetal: Fetal HR 150, reactive NST. No decelerations.

hidden_data.cmp: AST 350, ALT 300 (severely elevated - liver involvement), Total Bilirubin 2.5, LDH 800, Haptoglobin <10 (hemolysis). Platelets 65,000 (thrombocytopenia). Creatinine 1.3, Uric Acid 8.5. Proteinuria 3+ (2.5g/24h).
hidden_data.cbc: Hb 9.5 (hemolytic anemia), Schistocytes on peripheral smear (microangiopathic hemolytic anemia), Platelets 65,000
hidden_data.coagulation: PT 14, INR 1.2, PTT 32, Fibrinogen 350 (normal - differentiates from DIC)
hidden_data.ob_us: Fetal growth at 20th percentile. Normal AFI. Normal Dopplers. BPP 8/8.
hidden_data.lfts: AST 350, ALT 300. No RUQ mass on US (no hepatic hematoma).

correct_diagnosis: HELLP Syndrome (Hemolysis, Elevated Liver Enzymes, Low Platelets) - Class 2 (Platelets 50-100K) at 32 Weeks Gestation
key_learning_points: HELLP = severe form of preeclampsia. H: Hemolysis (microangiopathic - schistocytes, LDH >600, Haptoglobin low), EL: Elevated Liver enzymes (AST/ALT >70, often >500), LP: Low Platelets (<100K). Occurs in 10-20% of severe preeclampsia. Can present WITHOUT hypertension or proteinuria (15%)

Clinical: RUQ/epigastric pain (liver capsule distension - ominous sign of hepatic hematoma/rupture), Nausea/vomiting, Malaise (flu-like symptoms), Headache, Visual changes. Differential: TTP (normal LFTs, neuro symptoms), AFLP (Acute Fatty Liver of Pregnancy: hypoglycemia, DIC, encephalopathy, jaundice)
Classification: Class 1: Platelets <50K (severe). Class 2: Platelets 50-100K (moderate). Class 3: Platelets 100-150K (mild). Mississippi classification includes LDH and AST criteria

Management: DELIVERY regardless of gestational age. Only cure is delivery of placenta. If ≥34 weeks: Deliver immediately. If <34 weeks: Betamethasone for fetal lung maturity (12mg IM q24h ×2 doses). Deliver after steroids complete (48h) IF maternal status stable. If maternal deterioration (platelets dropping, LFTs rising, renal failure, DIC, eclampsia, hepatic hematoma) = DELIVER URGENTLY regardless of gestational age

Perioperative: Platelets <50K: Transfuse before C-section (goal >50K). Platelets <100K: NO epidural/spinal (risk of epidural hematoma → paralysis). General anesthesia for C-section. Postpartum: Continue MgSO4 ×24h. Close monitoring: Platelets, LFTs, renal function. Platelets nadir 24-48h postpartum. LFTs improve by 3-5 days. Dexamethasone for severe thrombocytopenia (controversial - may transiently improve platelets but no mortality benefit)

Complications: Hepatic hematoma/rupture (RUQ pain + shock - surgical emergency), DIC, Renal failure, Pulmonary edema, Eclampsia, Retinal detachment, Subcapsular liver hematoma. Maternal mortality 1-5% (higher with hepatic rupture). Recurrence: 3-5% in subsequent pregnancies
